name: Highlights of Indochina: Vietnam, Cambodia & Thailand 3 weeks
duration: Places:Hanoi - Halong Bay - Hoi An - Ho Chi Minh City - Mekong Delta - Phnom Penh - Siem Reap - Chiang Mai - Bangkok
highlights: A special itinerary suitable for all ages to explore the Indochina within 3 weeks.
Explore the stunning iconic destinations from Vietnam to Cambodia, and end the wonderful journey in Thailand.
An opportunity to taste local specialties, interact with friendly people, and immerse yourself in the peaceful life of the villages.
The best choice for anyone seeking a long vacation traveling through Vietnam, Cambodia, and Thailand at affordable prices.

itinerary.title: Hanoi Arrival
itinerary.description:
Morning

After landing at Noi Bai International Airport, our guide and driver will pick you up and transfer you to the hotel. Afternoon

As this is a free day, you can choose to stay in your room to relax after a long flight or try some optional activities to experience like a local. Hanoi Street Food Walking Tour

	

From US $29/pax




Hanoi Cooking Class Half Day

	

From US $48/pax

NOTE:

If you are interested, you can contact our travel consultant to book these additional services. The check-in time is 14:00, you can book for early check-in with an additional fee. Arrive in Hanoi and rel...

itinerary.title: Half-day Hanoi City Tour
itinerary.description:
Morning/ Afternoon

You will be picked up at 8:00 - 8:30 and start exploring the first destination - Hanoi Capital. Don’t miss out on these highlight spots. Temple of Literature: The first university of Vietnam to honor Confucius and store long-lasting cultural & historical relics dating back to the 11th century

One Pillar Pagoda: A Buddhist pagoda designed in unique structure representing the Vietnamese national flower - the lotus

Ho Chi Minh’s Mausoleum: A sacred place where the greatest Vietnamese leader rests in peace

Tran Quoc Pagoda: A 1,500-year-old stupa in the shape of a nine-story...

itinerary.title: Halong Bay Overnight Cruise
itinerary.description:
Morning

8:00 - 8:30: Hit the road from Hanoi Old Quarter (your hotel or meeting point), you will head for Halong Bay cruise port to join the 2-day cruise. On the way, you will have a break at a pearl workshop to see how the locals produce and buy some pearl jewelry if you like. Arrive at the cruise lounge at around 11:30, the staff will help you check in and give you the cabin key as well as your luggage tag. Around 12:00, it’s time to embark on your cruise or transfer to the ship by tender. You will receive a warm hello and welcome drink onboard

itinerary.title: Halong Bay - Hanoi - Flight to Da Nang
itinerary.description:
Morning

Breath in. Breathe out. Let’s welcome a new day with a short Tai Chi class and admire the stunning sunrise over Halong Bay. After breakfast, you will follow the cruise itinerary to explore deeper the bay. Around 9:30 - 10:00, you’ll come back to the ship and pack your luggage to process check out

itinerary.title: Half-day Hoi An Town Discovery
itinerary.description:
Morning/ Afternoon

Begin at Hoi An Market, where vibrant stalls, fragrant street food, and lively crowds offer a truly immersive shopping experience

From there, head to Fujian Assembly Hall, adorned with colorful gates, dragon sculptures, and intricate rooftops; take a moment to observe locals making offerings at family altars. Next, visit Phung Hung Ancient House and Tan Ky Ancient House, two historic homes that have gracefully survived over 200 years of weather and change. Afterward, explore the Museum of Folk Culture in Hoi An, located in a 150-year-old Chinese merchant house, featuring a...

itinerary.title: Free Day in Hoi An
itinerary.description:
You can freely explore Hoi An on this day. Exploring the outskirts is a great idea, for example, visiting the famous ruined sanctuary of the Champa Empire, learning to be a farmer, or riding a motorbike through the countryside. Here are some recommended day tours you should consider:

My Son Sanctuary Half-day Tour 

	

From US $48/pax




Farming Tour in Cam Thanh Village

	

From US $79/pax




Hoi An Vespa Half-day Tour

	

From US $120/pax

Act As A Farmer In Cam Thanh Village

itinerary.title: Flight to Ho Chi Minh City
itinerary.description: After breakfast, you will be picked up and transferred to Da Nang Airport and get on a 1.5-hour flight to Ho Chi Minh City. Our driver will wait for you at the airport and drive you to your hotel. It’s time to freely explore the city or just take a nap.

itinerary.title: Half-day Ho Chi Minh City Tour
itinerary.description:
Depart from your hotel at 8:00 and start a half-day city tour to explore highlight spots in Saigon. Reunification Palace: A remarkable building of Parisian 1960s architecture, which stores a great source of historical relics since the Vietnam War

Saigon Notre Dame Cathedral & Post Office: Outstanding French-built sites which feature exquisite neoclassical architecture

War Remnants Museum: A unique museum that exhibits documentaries, photos, videos, and relics about war crimes that impacted the Vietnamese

Local market: The final stop where you not only can buy souvenirs but also learn how to...
itinerary.accommodation: Hotel in Ho Chi Minh City

itinerary.title: Mekong Delta - Cai Be - Can Tho
itinerary.description:
Morning

Start heading for the Mekong Delta and reach Cai Be. Let’s get on the sampan boat and admire the scenic life along the riverside. Your first stop will be a local factory where a family production makes a range of traditional products like coconut candy, rice paper, pop rice, wine, etc. Here, you can try a bite of these specialties. Continue discovering the next stop, the world of mangrove swamps and tropical fruits - Tan Phong Island is waiting for you

itinerary.title: Cai Rang Floating Market - Ho Chi Minh City
itinerary.description:
Morning

Start the day with a transfer from your hotel to the pier and board a traditional wooden boat for a trip to the Cai Rang floating market, one of the most active and colorful markets in the Mekong Delta. Along the way, you’ll see locals going about their daily lives on the river, trading fruits, vegetables, and other goods straight from their boats. The journey also includes a visit to a small noodle workshop, where you can watch how noodles are made, and a chance to sample fresh tropical fruits during the cruise. This experience offers an authentic glimpse into the river-based lifesty...
itinerary.accommodation: Hotel in Ho Chi Minh City

itinerary.title: Ho Chi Minh City - Flight to Siem Reap
itinerary.description: Enjoy a leisurely morning in Ho Chi Minh City — take time to savor a final Vietnamese coffee, pick up souvenirs at a local market, or simply relax before your departure. At the scheduled time, a private transfer will take you to the airport for your international flight to Siem Reap, Cambodia — the gateway to the magnificent temples of Angkor. Upon arrival, you’ll be greeted by our guide and transferred to your hotel. The evening is free for you to unwind or explore the city’s lively atmosphere at your own pace.

itinerary.title: Angkor Wat Full-Day Tour
itinerary.description:
Morning

After breakfast, embark on an unforgettable full-day journey through the majestic Angkor Archaeological Park, a vast complex that stands as the spiritual and historical heart of Cambodia. Your exploration begins at Angkor Thom, the ancient walled capital of the Khmer Empire dating back to the 12th century. You’ll enter through the imposing South Gate, where a dramatic stone causeway is flanked by towering figures reenacting the Hindu myth of the Churning of the Ocean of Milk, a powerful symbol of balance and renewal. Within the city walls, delve into the mysterious beauty of the Bayon...

itinerary.title: Banteay Srei - Pradark Village
itinerary.description: After breakfast, embark on a scenic 35-kilometer drive northeast of Siem Reap to visit the jewel of Angkorian artistry - Banteay Srei. Often referred to as the "Citadel of Women," this temple is renowned for its delicate, intricate carvings and its unique pink sandstone construction. Built in the 10th century by Yajnavaraha, a royal scholar and spiritual advisor to King Jayavarman V, Banteay Srei is a true masterpiece of Khmer architecture. Its detailed bas-reliefs and miniature scale set it apart from other Angkorian temples, showcasing some of the finest stonework in Southeast Asia. On your ...

itinerary.title: Siem Reap Flight to Chiang Mai
itinerary.description:
Morning & Afternoon

Enjoy a leisurely morning in Siem Reap. Take your time to pack, relax by the pool, or explore the nearby markets for any last-minute souvenirs. It’s a perfect opportunity to soak in the final moments of Cambodia at your own pace. At the scheduled time, transfer to the airport for your flight to Chiang Mai, Thailand. Upon arrival at Chiang Mai Airport, you’ll be warmly welcomed by your driver and transferred to your hotel in the city center

itinerary.title: Chiang Mai - Cooking Class Half Day
itinerary.description: Today, dive into the rich culinary heritage of Northern Thailand with a half-day Thai cooking class - a hands-on, flavorful experience. Head to the cooking class, where your instructor will introduce you to fresh herbs, spices, and seasonal produce essential to Thai cuisine. Under the guidance of an experienced chef, you’ll prepare several authentic dishes such as: Pad Thai (Stir-fried noodles), Mango Sticky Rice, ... Return to your hotel with new culinary skills. Spend your free time relaxing or exploring Chiang Mai at your leisure.

itinerary.title: Full-day Discover Hill Tribes & Artisans Experience
itinerary.description:
Morning

In the early morning, our guide will pick you up and head directly to the Karen Village, Mae Rim Area, where the long-neck women are known for wearing golden rings around their necks. This symbol of beauty was used as a way of protection from tiger attacks in the past. According to Karen hill tribes, the longer the neck the prettier the woman is. Continue to visit other hill tribes with elephant camps in the same area. Here, you can learn more about the ethnic lifestyle and culture, as well as their farming techniques

itinerary.title: Flight to Bangkok Capital
itinerary.description: This day is for transportation and relaxation. You will take fly directly to Bangkok in about 1.25 hours. Our driver will be waiting for you at the airport and take you to your hotel. Different from the laid-back Chiang Mai, Bangkok is more hustle and bustle with vibrant street life and amusement. You should take time to explore the nightlife and street food in the evening

itinerary.title: Full-day Explore Bangkok Highlights
itinerary.description:
Morning

8:00: Start the morning trip with Royal Grand Palace, a former royal residence in Bangkok. With more than 30 royal buildings, temples, museums, and countless statues, this complex is the most popular tourist attraction and largest pilgrimage destination for devout Buddhists in Bangkok. Here, you can visit lots of famous sites that belong to or have ties to the Thailand royal family, such as Wat Phra Kaew with the glorious Emerald Buddha, the Eight Colored Towers, the Pantheon of the Chakri Kings, and the Golden Chedi. Only a 10-minute walk from Grand Palace, the next stop is Wat Pho, ...

itinerary.title: Full-day Ayutthaya Discovery
itinerary.description:
Morning

Enjoy your breakfast before the car comes and picks you up at 8.00 - 8.30. Let’s go to Ayutthaya which is over 80 km far from Bangkok. On the way to Ayutthaya, stop by the Bang Pa-in Summer Place, which belongs to the royal family and is used occasionally for receptions and banquets. Arrive at Ayutthaya by lunch, you will have leisure time to enjoy your meal at a local restaurant. Afternoon

Start exploring this ancient city with these impressive temples

itinerary.title: Free Day in Bangkok
itinerary.description:
Today is the last free day of this tour package. There are a ton of things to do in Bangkok: go shopping in Siam, book a boat tour to the floating market, or join us on these interesting day tours for unique experiences:

Jim Thompson House & Museum Half Day

	

From US $65/pax




Thonburi Klong Tour Half Day

	

From US $70/pax




Bangkok Street Food Tour By Tuk Tuk

	

From US $62/pax

Jim Thompson House & Museum Massive Collection Of Thai Art

itinerary.title: Bangkok Departure
itinerary.description: Have breakfast at the hotel and enjoy your last free time in Thailand. Following the flight time, our driver comes after and takes you to the airport. This is the end of our long journey together. As your travel agent, we are honored to accompany you on your Indochina holiday package. We hope that you had a great time with us and we looking forward to serve you on future trips!

included: Overnight in shared Twin/ Double  room with Air-condition (AC) at hotel.
All transport during tour on air - conditioned vehicle as per itinerary (including airport pick up & see off)
All in-tour stationed local English-speaking guide.
Meals as indicated in the program
2 domestic flights Hanoi -  Danang ; Danang - Ho Chi Minh;
3 International flights: Ho Chi Minh - Siem Reap, Siem Reap - Chiang Mai & Chiang Mai - Bangkok (20kg Checked luggage)
